260622bmk1_RNAi

Sam Zavislan-Pullaro

2026-06-22

  • load libraries
  • Import the data that was outputted from the new macro:
  • Create annotation columns
  • Skip peak alignment - use fixed coordinate instead
  • Generate .csv of max intensities for erm-1 in bmk-1 KD and L4440 control
  • re-arrange the dataset and calculate the means
  • Visualize how normalization is affecting y-axis
  • Try to create a metric of “membrane-y-ness” for each condition combo
  • List by order of max intensity in channel 2 (erm-1)
  • Calculate the statistics
  • Session info

load libraries

library(tidyverse)
library(gridExtra)
library(data.table)
library(rstatix)

Import the data that was outputted from the new macro:

Note:

channel 1 = set-3

channel 2 = erm-1

# import the control data
# control data comes from L4440 empty vector RNAi controls 
control_1 <- read.table(file = "../01_input/2026-6-22_1458_datafile_for_230713_L4440_RNAi_rep1.txt", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")
control_2 <- read.table(file = "../01_input/2026-6-22_153_datafile_for_230828_L4440_RNAi_rep2.txt", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")
control_3 <- read.table(file = "../01_input/2026-6-22_156_datafile_for_240122_LP306_L4440-rep3.txt", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")


# import RNAi treatment data 
test_1 <- read.table(file = "../01_input/2026-6-22_1528_datafile_for_220719_LP306_bmk-1_RNAi.txt", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")
test_2 <- read.table(file = "../01_input/2026-6-22_1536_datafile_for_230408_LP306_BMK-1_rep1.txt", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")
test_3 <- read.table(file = "../01_input/2026-6-22_1544_datafile_for_231107_LP306_BMK-1_rep2.txt", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")
test_4 <- read.table(file = "../01_input/2026-6-22_1550_datafile_for_231113_LP306_BMK-1_rep3.txt", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")

# test its dimensions
#dim(control_1)
#dim(control_2)
#dim(test_1)
#dim(test_2)
#dim(test_3)
#dim(test_4)

# Rename the column names
col_times <- paste(rep(1:333), sep = "")
colnames(control_1) <- c("file", "channel", col_times)
colnames(control_2) <- c("file", "channel", col_times)
colnames(control_3) <- c("file", "channel", col_times)

colnames(test_1) <- c("file", "channel", col_times)
colnames(test_2) <- c("file", "channel", col_times)
colnames(test_3) <- c("file", "channel", col_times)
colnames(test_4) <- c("file", "channel", col_times)

# Check that the dimensions remain unchanged
dim(control_1)
## [1]  11 337
dim(control_2)
## [1]   5 337
dim(control_3)
## [1]   9 337
dim(test_1)
## [1]   7 337
dim(test_2)
## [1]  15 337
dim(test_3)
## [1]   9 337
dim(test_4)
## [1]   7 337
# control_1[1:9,1:8]
# control_2[1:9,1:8]
# control_3[1:9,1:8]

# test_1[1:9,1:8]
# test_2[1:9,1:8]
# test_3[1:9,1:8]
# test_4[1:9,1:8]

Create annotation columns

# Extract out the salient information from the filename and pivot the data longer - "permed" version
# Had to manually change image names to follow convention/consistency

control_1_exp <- control_1[2:dim(control_1)[1],1:335] %>%
  separate_wider_delim(file, delim = "_", names = c("date", "strain", "treatment", "embryoID", NA)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = `1`:`333`, names_to = "xpoint", values_to = "intensity") %>%
  mutate(unique_id = paste(date, strain, treatment, embryoID, channel, sep = "_"))
 
control_2_exp <- control_2[2:dim(control_2)[1],1:335] %>%
  separate_wider_delim(file, delim = "_", names = c("date", "strain", "treatment", "embryoID", NA)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = `1`:`333`, names_to = "xpoint", values_to = "intensity") %>%
  mutate(unique_id = paste(date, strain, treatment, embryoID, channel, sep = "_"))
 
control_3_exp <- control_3[2:dim(control_3)[1],1:335] %>%
  separate_wider_delim(file, delim = "_", names = c("date", "strain", "treatment", "embryoID", NA)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = `1`:`333`, names_to = "xpoint", values_to = "intensity") %>%
  mutate(unique_id = paste(date, strain, treatment, embryoID, channel, sep = "_")) 

 
test_1_exp <- test_1[2:dim(test_1)[1],1:335] %>%
  separate_wider_delim(file, delim = "_", names = c("date", "strain", "treatment", "embryoID", NA)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = `1`:`333`, names_to = "xpoint", values_to = "intensity") %>%
  mutate(unique_id = paste(date, strain, treatment, embryoID, channel, sep = "_"))

test_2_exp <- test_2[2:dim(test_2)[1],1:335] %>%
  separate_wider_delim(file, delim = "_", names = c("date", "strain", "treatment", "embryoID", NA)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = `1`:`333`, names_to = "xpoint", values_to = "intensity") %>%
  mutate(unique_id = paste(date, strain, treatment, embryoID, channel, sep = "_"))

test_3_exp <- test_3[2:dim(test_3)[1],1:335] %>%
  separate_wider_delim(file, delim = "_", names = c("date", "strain", "treatment", "embryoID", NA)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = `1`:`333`, names_to = "xpoint", values_to = "intensity") %>%
  mutate(unique_id = paste(date, strain, treatment, embryoID, channel, sep = "_"))

test_4_exp <- test_4[2:dim(test_4)[1],1:335] %>%
  separate_wider_delim(file, delim = "_", names = c("date", "strain", "treatment", "embryoID", NA)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = `1`:`333`, names_to = "xpoint", values_to = "intensity") %>%
  mutate(unique_id = paste(date, strain, treatment, embryoID, channel, sep = "_"))
# Add in the timepoints

addInTimepoints <- function(tibble) {
  timepoints <- as.vector(unlist(control_1[1,3:335]))
  repnumb = dim(tibble)[1]/333
  tibble::add_column(tibble, timepoints = rep(timepoints,repnumb), .after = "xpoint")
}

control_1_plustime <- addInTimepoints(control_1_exp)
control_2_plustime <- addInTimepoints(control_2_exp)
control_3_plustime <- addInTimepoints(control_3_exp)

test_1_plustime <- addInTimepoints(test_1_exp)
test_2_plustime <- addInTimepoints(test_2_exp)
test_3_plustime <- addInTimepoints(test_3_exp)
test_4_plustime <- addInTimepoints(test_4_exp)

#check it
control_1_plustime[330:340,]

# Merge the datasets together 
bmk1_RNAi_data_total <- rbind(control_1_plustime, control_2_plustime, control_3_plustime, test_1_plustime, test_2_plustime, test_3_plustime, test_4_plustime)

# Plot it - all samples
ggplot(data = bmk1_RNAi_data_total, aes(x = timepoints, y = intensity, group = c(unique_id))) +
  geom_line(aes(colour = unique_id))+
  facet_wrap(~ channel + treatment)+
  guides(color = "none")+
  theme_bw()

Skip peak alignment - use fixed coordinate instead

Alignment step removed (prior discussion: aligning each embryo to its own observed channel-2 peak risks manufacturing apparent enrichment at the alignment point, since by construction that point is the brightest value in every embryo). Instead, every embryo uses the same fixed coordinate system based on its row position (xpoint), recentered so it spans the same -166:166 range the old aligned coordinate used. This keeps every downstream chunk (normalization, wide pivot, stats, plots, membraneyness calc) working on a column still called aligned_row, with no other code changes required.

# No channel split needed here anymore - alignment was the only reason
# channel 1 and channel 2 were ever separated at this stage. Both channels
# get the same fixed coordinate, so we recenter the whole dataset at once.

bmk1_dt <- as.data.table(bmk1_RNAi_data_total)
bmk1_dt$xpoint <- as.integer(bmk1_dt$xpoint)

# Fixed coordinate: recenter xpoint (1:333) to run -166:166, identical for
# every embryo and every channel - no peak-finding, no per-embryo offset.
bmk1_dt[, aligned_row := xpoint - 167]

# Check that everything worked
dim(bmk1_RNAi_data_total)
## [1] 18648     9
dim(bmk1_dt)
## [1] 18648    10
range(bmk1_dt$aligned_row)
## [1] -166  166
# Rename to match the variable name the rest of the script expects
total_align_long <- bmk1_dt %>%
  select(strain, aligned_row, unique_id, intensity)
total_align_long %>%
  group_by(aligned_row) %>%
  summarize(
    n_total = n(),
    n_na = sum(is.na(intensity)),
    pct_na = mean(is.na(intensity)) * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(aligned_row) %>%
  print(n = Inf)

Generate .csv of max intensities for erm-1 in bmk-1 KD and L4440 control

max_intensities <- total_align_long %>%
  filter(!grepl("ch1", unique_id)) %>% # filter out rows that contain set-3 in ch1
  group_by(unique_id) %>%
  slice_max(intensity, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)

write_csv(max_intensities, "bmk-1_L4440_ch2_max_intensities.csv")

re-arrange the dataset and calculate the means

# Normalize the data by dividing by the embryo-specific mean
bmk1_norm_total <- total_align_long %>%
  separate_wider_delim(unique_id, delim = "_", names = c("date", NA, "treatment", "embryoID", "channel")) %>%
  group_by(date, embryoID, channel, treatment) %>%
  mutate(normalized_intensity = intensity / mean(intensity, na.rm = TRUE))
table(bmk1_norm_total$channel, bmk1_norm_total$treatment)
##      
##       bmk-1 L4440
##   ch1  5661  3663
##   ch2  5661  3663
head(bmk1_norm_total)
## # A tibble: 6 × 8
## # Groups:   date, embryoID, channel, treatment [1]
##   strain aligned_row date   treatment embryoID channel intensity
##   <chr>        <dbl> <chr>  <chr>     <chr>    <chr>       <dbl>
## 1 LP306         -166 230713 L4440     03       ch2         4856.
## 2 LP306         -165 230713 L4440     03       ch2         4933.
## 3 LP306         -164 230713 L4440     03       ch2         4977.
## 4 LP306         -163 230713 L4440     03       ch2         5013.
## 5 LP306         -162 230713 L4440     03       ch2         5069.
## 6 LP306         -161 230713 L4440     03       ch2         5138.
## # ℹ 1 more variable: normalized_intensity <dbl>

Visualize how normalization is affecting y-axis

ggplot(data = bmk1_norm_total, aes(x = aligned_row, y = normalized_intensity, 
                                     group = interaction(date, embryoID, channel))) +
  geom_line(aes(color = interaction(date, embryoID, channel)), alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ channel + treatment) +
  guides(color = "none") +
  theme_bw()

# Separate out the unique identifier info, then pivot sider
bmk1_wide_by_sample <- bmk1_norm_total %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = c(date, embryoID, channel, treatment), 
            values_from = normalized_intensity,
            id_cols = c(strain, aligned_row))


# Second Try - Separate out the unique identifier info, then pivot sider
bmk1_wide_by_sample <- bmk1_norm_total %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = c(date, embryoID), 
            values_from = normalized_intensity,
            id_cols = c(strain, aligned_row, treatment, channel))



colnames(bmk1_wide_by_sample)
##  [1] "strain"      "aligned_row" "treatment"   "channel"     "230713_03"  
##  [6] "230713_04"   "230713_08"   "230713_10"   "230713_12"   "230828_05"  
## [11] "230828_07"   "240122_01"   "240122_06"   "240122_08"   "240122_09"  
## [16] "220719_01"   "220719_02"   "220719_09"   "230408_04"   "230408_08"  
## [21] "230408_10"   "230408_12"   "230408_13"   "230408_14"   "230408_15"  
## [26] "231107_01"   "231107_02"   "231107_06"   "231107_10"   "231113_04"  
## [31] "231113_05"   "231113_06"
# Play around with mean, medium, and sum:
bmk1_wide_with_stats <- bmk1_wide_by_sample %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    sum_signal    = sum(c_across(`230713_03`:`231113_06`), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_signal   = mean(c_across(`230713_03`:`231113_06`), na.rm = TRUE),
    median_signal = median(c_across(`230713_03`:`231113_06`), na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_signal     = sd(c_across(`230713_03`:`231113_06`), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# colnames(bmk1_wide_with_stats)

bmk1_wide_with_range <- bmk1_wide_with_stats %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(ymeanmax = sum(mean_signal, sd_signal) ) %>%
  mutate(ymeanmin = sum(mean_signal, -sd_signal)) 

colorselection = c("#000000", "#2189ce")

# Plot split by RNAi treatment and L4440 empty vector control 
a <- ggplot(data = bmk1_wide_with_range, aes(x = aligned_row, y = mean_signal, group = interaction(channel, treatment)))+
  geom_line(aes(color=channel))+
  scale_color_manual(values = colorselection, aesthetics = c("colour", "fill"), labels = c("set-1 mRNA", "erm-1 mRNA"))+
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ymeanmin, ymax = ymeanmax, fill=channel), alpha = 0.2, ) +
  facet_wrap(~ treatment)+
  theme_bw()

a

# Plot in a different way - set-3 and erm-1 mRNA split into facets
colorselection = c("#2189ce", "#000000")
channel.labs <- c("set-3 mRNA", "erm-1 mRNA")
names(channel.labs) <- c("ch1", "ch2")

# Plot split by transcript
b <- ggplot(data = bmk1_wide_with_range, aes(x = aligned_row, y = mean_signal, group = interaction(channel, treatment)))+
  geom_line(aes(color=treatment))+
  scale_color_manual(values = colorselection, aesthetics = c("colour", "fill"), labels = c("control", "bmk-1 RNAi"))+
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ymeanmin, ymax = ymeanmax, fill=treatment), alpha = 0.2, ) +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, labeller = labeller(channel = channel.labs))+
  theme_bw()

Try to create a metric of “membrane-y-ness” for each condition combo

I want a metric that reports the ratio of erm-1 intensity signal at “0” (the peak) versus at -100 or +100. I’ll take the average of -100 and +100.

# extract the "peak" at 1 versus the -100 and +100 values
peaks_and_valleys <- bmk1_norm_total %>%
  filter(aligned_row %in% c(-100,1,100))

# Nest the -100, 1, and +100 values into a single cell
nest_pandv <- peaks_and_valleys %>%
  group_by(date, treatment, embryoID, channel) %>%
  nest()

# A function to calculate the ratio of the value at 1 compared to the mean of -100 and +100
my_calc2 <- function(df) {
  df$normalized_intensity[2] / mean(c(df$normalized_intensity[1], df$normalized_intensity[3]))
}

# Calculate the ratio for each datapoint
foldChange_calc <- nest_pandv %>%
  mutate(fc_enrich = map_dbl(data, my_calc2))

# Change some columns to factors
foldChange_calc$treatment <- as.factor(foldChange_calc$treatment)
foldChange_calc$channel <- as.factor(foldChange_calc$channel)

# calculate the max & min - figure out how to set the y-axis
summary(log(foldChange_calc$fc_enrich, base = 2))
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -0.03987  0.14913  0.25947  0.34568  0.49720  1.15146
# plot the data:
d <- ggplot(data = foldChange_calc, aes(x = as.factor(channel), y = log(fc_enrich, base = 2), fill = treatment))+
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA)+
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitterdodge())+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.5, 1.5)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("L4440" = "darkgray", "bmk-1" = "lightblue"),
                   labels = c("L4440" = "L4440 control", "bmk-1" = "bmk-1 RNAi"))+labs(y = "log2 FoldChange Enrichment of mRNA \n(mRNA intensity at 0 / mean mRNA intensity at -100 and 100)", x = "mRNA") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("ch1" = "set-3", "ch2" = "erm-1"))+
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

d

e <- ggplot(data = foldChange_calc, aes(x = as.factor(treatment), y = log(fc_enrich, base = 2), fill = channel))+
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA)+
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitterdodge())+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.5, 1.5)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(y = "log2 FoldChange Enrichment of mRNA \n(mRNA intensity at 0 / mean mRNA intensity at -100 and 100)", x = "mRNA") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

e

getwd()
## [1] "/Users/samzavislan/Desktop/onish/people/naly/projects/erm-1_LP306_RNAi/SZP260525_R_analysis/260622_newMacros/bmk-1_L4440_newMacro/02_scripts"
library(dplyr)

foldChange_calc %>%
  group_by(treatment, channel) %>%
  summarise(
    min_val    = min(log(fc_enrich, base = 2), na.rm = TRUE),
    median_val = median(log(fc_enrich, base = 2), na.rm = TRUE),
    max_val    = max(log(fc_enrich, base = 2), na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## `summarise()` has regrouped the output.
## ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by treatment and channel.
## ℹ Output is grouped by treatment.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(treatment, channel))` for per-operation grouping
##   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.
## # A tibble: 4 × 5
## # Groups:   treatment [2]
##   treatment channel  min_val median_val max_val
##   <fct>     <fct>      <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 bmk-1     ch1     -0.0399       0.150   0.602
## 2 bmk-1     ch2      0.00307      0.387   1.07 
## 3 L4440     ch1     -0.00126      0.168   0.391
## 4 L4440     ch2      0.351        0.638   1.15
foldChange_calc %>%
  mutate(log2_fc = log(fc_enrich, base = 2)) %>%
  group_by(treatment, channel) %>%
  slice(c(
    which.min(log2_fc),
    which(log2_fc == median(log2_fc)), 
    which.max(log2_fc)
  ))
## # A tibble: 12 × 7
## # Groups:   treatment, channel [4]
##    date   treatment embryoID channel data             fc_enrich  log2_fc
##    <chr>  <fct>     <chr>    <fct>   <list>               <dbl>    <dbl>
##  1 230408 bmk-1     13       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     0.973 -0.0399 
##  2 231113 bmk-1     04       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.11   0.150  
##  3 220719 bmk-1     01       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.52   0.602  
##  4 230408 bmk-1     13       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.00   0.00307
##  5 231113 bmk-1     05       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.31   0.387  
##  6 231107 bmk-1     10       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     2.10   1.07   
##  7 230713 L4440     12       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     0.999 -0.00126
##  8 230713 L4440     03       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.12   0.168  
##  9 240122 L4440     08       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.31   0.391  
## 10 230828 L4440     05       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.28   0.351  
## 11 240122 L4440     09       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.56   0.638  
## 12 240122 L4440     01       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     2.22   1.15
df <- foldChange_calc %>%
  mutate(log2_fc = log(fc_enrich, base = 2)) %>%
  group_by(treatment, channel) %>%
  mutate(med = median(log2_fc))

label_points <- bind_rows(
  df %>% slice(which.min(log2_fc))              %>% mutate(label = "min"),
  df %>% slice(which.min(abs(log2_fc - med)))   %>% mutate(label = "median"),
  df %>% slice(which.max(log2_fc))              %>% mutate(label = "max")
)
foldChange_calc %>%
  mutate(log2_fc = log(fc_enrich, base = 2)) %>%
  group_by(treatment, channel) %>%
  filter(log2_fc == min(log2_fc) | log2_fc == max(log2_fc))
## # A tibble: 8 × 7
## # Groups:   treatment, channel [4]
##   date   treatment embryoID channel data             fc_enrich  log2_fc
##   <chr>  <fct>     <chr>    <fct>   <list>               <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 230713 L4440     12       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     0.999 -0.00126
## 2 230828 L4440     05       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.28   0.351  
## 3 240122 L4440     01       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     2.22   1.15   
## 4 240122 L4440     08       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.31   0.391  
## 5 220719 bmk-1     01       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.52   0.602  
## 6 230408 bmk-1     13       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.00   0.00307
## 7 230408 bmk-1     13       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     0.973 -0.0399 
## 8 231107 bmk-1     10       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     2.10   1.07
library(ggrepel)

df <- foldChange_calc %>%
  mutate(log2_fc = log(fc_enrich, base = 2)) %>%
  group_by(treatment, channel) %>%
  mutate(med = median(log2_fc))

label_points <- bind_rows(
  df %>% slice(which.min(log2_fc))            %>% mutate(label = "min"),
  df %>% slice(which.min(abs(log2_fc - med))) %>% mutate(label = "median"),
  df %>% slice(which.max(log2_fc))            %>% mutate(label = "max")
) %>%
  # Keep only one label per row — if min/median/max land on same row, 
  # priority: min > max > median
  group_by(treatment, channel, date, embryoID) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup()

List by order of max intensity in channel 2 (erm-1)

label_points <- bind_rows(
  df %>% slice(which.min(log2_fc)) %>% mutate(label = "min"),
  df %>% slice(which.max(log2_fc)) %>% mutate(label = "max"),
  df %>% filter(n() >= 3) %>% 
         slice(which.min(abs(log2_fc - med))) %>% mutate(label = "median")
) %>%
  distinct(treatment, channel, date, embryoID, .keep_all = TRUE)
library(ggrepel)

e + geom_label_repel(
  data = label_points,
  aes(x = as.factor(treatment), y = log2_fc,
      label = paste(label, paste(date, embryoID, sep = "_"), sep = ": "),
      fill = channel),
  position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),  # matches geom_boxplot default
  size = 3,
  alpha = 0.8,
  max.overlaps = 20,
  show.legend = FALSE
)

## Save the datasets


# set plotname
today <- format(Sys.Date(), "%y%m%d")
filename <- paste("../03_outputPlots/", today, "bmk-1_L4440.pdf", sep = "")

pdf(filename, width = 6, height = 6)
d
dev.off()

getwd()

Calculate the statistics

foldChange_calc
## # A tibble: 56 × 6
## # Groups:   date, treatment, embryoID, channel [56]
##    date   treatment embryoID channel data             fc_enrich
##    <chr>  <fct>     <chr>    <fct>   <list>               <dbl>
##  1 230713 L4440     03       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.56 
##  2 230713 L4440     03       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.12 
##  3 230713 L4440     04       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.40 
##  4 230713 L4440     04       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.11 
##  5 230713 L4440     08       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.74 
##  6 230713 L4440     08       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.18 
##  7 230713 L4440     10       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.38 
##  8 230713 L4440     10       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.18 
##  9 230713 L4440     12       ch2     <tibble [3 × 4]>     1.29 
## 10 230713 L4440     12       ch1     <tibble [3 × 4]>     0.999
## # ℹ 46 more rows
stats_wilcox <- foldChange_calc %>%
  group_by(channel) %>%
  wilcox_test(fc_enrich ~ treatment) %>%
  adjust_pvalue(method = "BH") %>%
  add_significance()

stats_wilcox
## # A tibble: 2 × 10
##   channel .y.     group1 group2    n1    n2 statistic      p  p.adj p.adj.signif
##   <fct>   <chr>   <chr>  <chr>  <int> <int>     <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> <chr>       
## 1 ch1     fc_enr… bmk-1  L4440     17    11        85 0.711  0.711  ns          
## 2 ch2     fc_enr… bmk-1  L4440     17    11        48 0.0326 0.0652 ns
### Run Welch's t-test to confirm same result 
stats_ttest <- foldChange_calc %>%
  group_by(channel) %>%
  t_test(fc_enrich ~ treatment) %>%
  group_by(channel) %>%              # re-add grouping, lost by t_test()
  adjust_pvalue(method = "BH") %>%
  add_significance()
stats_ttest
## # A tibble: 2 × 11
##   channel .y.       group1 group2    n1    n2 statistic    df      p  p.adj
##   <fct>   <chr>     <chr>  <chr>  <int> <int>     <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>
## 1 ch1     fc_enrich bmk-1  L4440     17    11  -0.00634  24.3 0.995  0.995 
## 2 ch2     fc_enrich bmk-1  L4440     17    11  -1.94     20.1 0.0662 0.0662
## # ℹ 1 more variable: p.adj.signif <chr>
